Hero.description: For over 50 years, Stellenbosch's Computer Science Department has been at the forefront of innovation — advancing research and developing leaders who drive technology and discovery globally.

Features.title: Why Stellenbosch CS?
Features.subtitle: Discover what makes our department a leader in computer science education and research.
Features.worldClassDescription: Undergraduate and postgraduate degrees designed to produce industry-ready graduates.
Features.researchDescription: Active research groups in AI, machine learning, data science, and cybersecurity.
Features.communityDescription: A collaborative environment connecting students, academics, and industry partners.
Features.globalDescription: Our alumni work at leading tech companies and research institutions worldwide.
Features.facultyDescription: Learn from internationally recognised researchers and experienced educators.
Features.handsOnDescription: Project-based curricula that prepare you for real-world software engineering challenges.

Programmes.title: Our Programmes
Programmes.subtitle: From undergraduate foundations to postgraduate research, find the right path for your career.
Programmes.bscDescription: A three-year undergraduate degree offering a strong foundation in algorithms, systems, and software engineering.
Programmes.honsDescription: An intensive honours year with advanced coursework and a research project in a specialist area.
Programmes.mscDescription: Postgraduate research programmes supervised by leading academics across multiple focus areas.

Contact.title: Get in Touch
Contact.description: Have questions about our programmes, research, or anything else? We'd love to hear from you.

UgModules.title: Undergraduate Modules
UgModules.subtitle: All Computer Science modules offered across the undergraduate programme, organised by year level.
UgModules.imageAlt: Students working in a computer lab

UgGuide.title: Programme Guide
UgGuide.subtitle: Everything you need to know about studying Computer Science at Stellenbosch University.
UgGuide.overviewText: BSc Computer Science is the primary option for students who want to pursue a career in software development. This international qualification enables you to work at national and international companies.
UgGuide.focalAreasSubtitle: Choose a specialisation that aligns with your interests and career goals.
UgGuide.generalCSDesc: A broad CS major combined with a choice of Science modules.
UgGuide.computerSystemsDesc: CS major combined with Electric and Electronic Engineering modules.
UgGuide.dataScienceDesc: CS major combined with Statistics and Data Science modules.
UgGuide.geneticsDesc: Computer Science with Genetics as a second major.
UgGuide.geoInfoDesc: Computer Science with GIT as a second major.
UgGuide.curriculumText: The CS core curriculum includes: CS 114, 144, 214, 244, 343, 344, and at least two of 314/313/315.

UgProspective.title: Prospective Undergraduate Students
UgProspective.subtitle: Everything you need to know about applying to study Computer Science at Stellenbosch University.
UgProspective.whatIsCSText: Computer Science is the theory and practice of algorithms. It is a wonderful combination of art, science, and engineering to solve problems with computers. Our students learn to program, develop, and manage large software systems — but most importantly, to frame problems in terms of computational processes.
whyCSReasons: Computing is part of everything we do
Make a positive difference in the world
Great opportunities for creativity and innovation
Essential part of well-rounded academic preparation
UgProspective.whyStellenboschAcademic: Stellenbosch University is amongst South Africa's leading tertiary institutions based on research output, student pass rates, and rated scientists.
UgProspective.whyStellenboschCommunity: Studying in Stellenbosch is more than just an education — it's a lifestyle. Great food and wine, stunning nature, a vibrant culture scene, and adventure activities all contribute to a well-balanced student experience.
UgProspective.admissionsText: Minimum admission requirements are listed in the Faculty of Science calendar. Note that Computer Science is popular and meeting the minimum requirements does not guarantee admission due to high demand and limited space.
UgProspective.afterGradText: Our graduates are sought after by companies internationally. Career paths include software engineering, data science, AI research, cybersecurity, consulting, and more.

PgModules.title: Postgraduate Modules
PgModules.subtitle: Modules offered across Honours, Masters, and PhD programmes in Computer Science.

PgProspective.title: Prospective Postgraduate Students
PgProspective.subtitle: Entry requirements, application procedures, and everything you need to know about joining our postgraduate programme.
honoursReqs: A degree that covers the CS curriculum
Average of at least 65% in final-year CS modules
At least 32 credits in quantitative modules at 2nd/3rd year level
PgProspective.mastersReqs: Honours degree in Computer Science or approved equivalent. Additional background may be required on a case-by-case basis.
PgProspective.phdReqs: A thesis-based Masters degree and the required Computer Science background.
howToApplySteps: For Masters/PhD: obtain agreement from a supervisor
Complete the application forms and upload required documentation
PgProspective.partTimeText: Part-time study is available. Honours can extend to two years; Masters to three-four years. We welcome international students — our modules are presented in English and are equivalent to 8 ECTS credits each.
PgProspective.fundingText: The university offers various bursaries and financial support. Teaching assistant positions can provide supplemental income. Some supervisors have research funds available for Masters/PhD students.

PgHonours.title: Honours in Computer Science
PgHonours.subtitle: A one-year full-time postgraduate programme that develops research skills and advanced CS knowledge.
PgHonours.overviewText: Over the last forty-five years, more than 600 talented students have passed through our programme. It is excellent preparation for pursuing either an MSc degree or a career in industry.
PgHonours.projectText: The honours project is worth 32 credits — a large software construction or research problem worked on independently under staff supervision throughout the year.
PgHonours.electivesText: Six elective modules of 16 credits each. Choose combinations focusing on Machine Learning, Data Science, Software Engineering, or a broader range.
PgHonours.maxExternal: At most two modules may be taken from related departments with permission.
highlights: Strong mix of theory and application courses
Independent research project with staff supervision
Graduates are highly sought after by industry
Can significantly impact your career trajectory

PgMasters.title: Masters in Computer Science
PgMasters.subtitle: A two-year thesis-based programme teaching world-class research skills.
PgMasters.overviewText: The goal is to teach students how to evaluate and conduct world-class research and become a master in their field. While the work is not required to be original cutting-edge research, it often is.
PgMasters.structureText: The Masters degree is based on thesis work under the guidance of a supervisor. You need the agreement of a staff member who will supervise your thesis.
howToStartSteps: Browse our Research pages to find areas of interest
Contact a potential supervisor to discuss topics
Obtain agreement from a supervisor before applying
If possible, arrange a face-to-face meeting
PgMasters.careerText: The reading, writing, and critical thinking skills acquired are highly sought after in industry. Most graduates who do not continue with a PhD go on to successful careers locally and abroad.

PgPhd.title: PhD in Computer Science
PgPhd.subtitle: Doctoral research producing original contributions to the field of Computer Science.
PgPhd.overviewText: A dissertation containing the results of your independent research is required. Our PhD programme offers the opportunity to work alongside internationally recognised researchers on cutting-edge problems.
PgPhd.requirementsText: A thesis-based Masters degree and the required Computer Science background. Additional requirements may be determined by your supervisor.
PgPhd.researchText: Our department has active research groups spanning artificial intelligence, machine learning, data science, cybersecurity, software engineering, and more.
PgPhd.supervisorText: Browse our Research pages and contact potential supervisors to discuss research topics. Obtaining a supervisor's agreement is essential before applying.

Metadata.title: Computer Science - Stellenbosch University
Metadata.description: Website for the Computer Science Department at Stellenbosch University. Explore our programmes, research, and community.

Research.title: Research
Research.subtitle: Explore the research groups and focus areas within the Department of Computer Science at Stellenbosch University.
Research.intro: Our department is home to several active research groups spanning theoretical computer science, software engineering, machine learning, and telecommunications. Faculty and students collaborate on problems that advance both the theory and practice of computing.
Research.automataTitle: Theory and Applications of Automata and Grammars
Research.automataDesc1: We are interested in the theory of nondeterministic finite automata (NFA), and in particular the descriptional complexity of symmetric difference NFA. More generally, we also consider the application of automata and grammars to a wide selection of problems, such as pattern layout optimization with cellular automata, image processing with cellular automata, music generation in the style of a given composer using probabilistic automata, and using probabilistic automata on parse trees of English sentences for automatic grammar correction of English text.
Research.automataDesc2: Automata and grammars have a rich and well-developed body of theory that can be applied, but often practice dictates that the theory should be extended in novel ways. For the pattern layout optimisation problem, for example, cellular automata with cell clustering, i.e., cellular automata with changes in neighborhood topology over time, were defined and shown to be advantageous in practical applications.
Research.automataLink1: Theory and Applications of Automata and Grammars
Research.sevDesc: The Software Engineering and Verification group at the University of Stellenbosch has been involved in the development of operating system kernels, protocols, and verification tools since 1990. We investigate the development of highly reliable system software by using a combination of computer-aided verification of designs, systematic testing, and defensive programming techniques.
Research.mlaiDesc: This group considers almost any aspect of the general decision-making problem, including sequential decision-making under uncertainty. Major sub-problems we consider are planning, machine learning, and search algorithms. Our approach is grounded in probability theory and game theory for managing uncertainty and multi-agent systems. We also investigate the combination of machine learning and big data, and specifically data from earth observation satellite sensors and radio interferometers.
Research.telkomDesc: The University of Stellenbosch Unit of the Telkom-Siemens Centre of Excellence in ATM and Broadband Networks and their Applications aim to promote research and development in broadband technologies and their applications and to train postgraduate students and professionals in the expertise required in this field of telecommunications.
